'''Damian Gibson''' (born 14 May 1975) is a former [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] international [[rugby league]] footballer. Primarily a {{rlp|fb}}, Gibson was the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] first ever try scorer.<ref name="therhinos.co.uk">{{Cite web |url=http://www.therhinos.co.uk/news/gibson-has-a-foot-in-both-camps-on-sunday-night-17300#.WRmXAGh97Dc |title=Leeds Rhinos | News |access-date=15 May 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180715015244/http://www.therhinos.co.uk/news/gibson-has-a-foot-in-both-camps-on-sunday-night-17300#.WRmXAGh97Dc |archive-date=15 July 2018 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

==Early life==
Gibson was born and raised in [[Rockhampton, Queensland]], Australia.

He attended [[Emmaus College, Rockhampton|Emmaus College]] before being signed by the [[Newcastle Knights]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://websites.sportstg.com/assoc_page.cgi?client=7-2245-0-0-0&sID=19459&&news_task=DETAIL&articleID=107848 |title=Damian Gibson - Warwick & District Junior Rugby League - SportsTG |website=websites.sportstg.com |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180122235048/http://websites.sportstg.com/assoc_page.cgi?client=7-2245-0-0-0&sID=19459&&news_task=DETAIL&articleID=107848 |archive-date=2018-01-22}} </ref>

==Playing career==
After spending 1994 with the [[Newcastle Knights]], where he represented the [[NSW City Origin|NSW City]] under-19 side, Gibson signed with the newly established [[North Queensland Cowboys]]. In Round 1 of the [[1995 ARL season]], Gibson made his first grade début as a 19-year-old in the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] first ever game. He scored the club's first ever try in the 16–32 loss to the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]], and was named in the Norwich Rising Star Team of 95.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/arl-1995/round-1/north-queensland-vs-sydney-bulldogs/summary.html|title = ARL 1995 - Round 1 - Rugby League Project}}</ref> He spent two seasons with the Cowboys, scoring 7 tries in 31 games and was the North Queensland Cowboys Top Tryscorer in 1996.

In 1997, Gibson joined the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]], scoring three tries and being named their Player of the Year.<ref name="therhinos.co.uk"/> He transferred to the [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax Blue Sox]] in 1998, where he spent the majority of his career. During his first stint with Halifax, he scored 40 tries in 113 games. In June 1998, he made his international début for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]], in a 15–12 loss to [[England national rugby league team|England]].

In 2002, he joined the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], spending one season at the [[The Willows, Salford|Willows]] before moving to the [[Castleford Tigers]] in 2003, following the Reds' relegation.<ref>{{cite web |title=Castleford sign Gibson |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/2405341.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=17 February 2024 |date=5 November 2002}}</ref> He re-joined Halifax in November 2004 after being released by the [[Castleford Tigers]] following their relegation from Super League and went on to score 15 tries in 30 appearances in 2005. After four more seasons at Halifax, Gibson joined the [[Sheffield Eagles]] in 2009, retiring at the end of the season.

==Post-playing career==
Following his retirement, Gibson served as an assistant coach for the [[Exiles rugby league team]], [[Leeds Rhinos]],<ref>{{cite web |title=Leeds Rhinos appoint Damian Gibson to coaching staff |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/leeds/9230801.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=17 February 2024 |date=26 November 2010}}</ref> [[Wales national rugby league team|Welsh national team]], and the [[Dewsbury Rams]]. While at the Rhinos, he coached the club's under-18 and under-20 sides and was on the coaching staff for their 2011 [[Super League XVI|Super League]] Grand Final winning season.<ref name="therhinos.co.uk"/>
